data.summary: Added TIERS const + routingHints.floor/source to TaskSchema (additive-optional, version stays literal 1), imported TIERS in config.ts eliminating the duplicatetier list, committed v1.3 fixture with loadGraph + resume-replay test (2 tasks P01-01/P01-02, 179/180 tests green), regenerated schema/rungraph.schema.json (diff-clean on re-run).

data.details:
4 commit(s):
schema/rungraph.schema.json      | 14 ++++++++-
 src/config/config.ts             |  3 +-
 src/graph/schema.ts              |  3 ++
 tests/fixtures/graph-v1.3.json   | 56 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/graph/schema.test.ts       | 31 +++++++++++++++++++
 tests/run/fixture-resume.test.ts | 66 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 6 files changed, 171 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

✓ TaskSchema accepts floor/source additively, rejects unknown tiers, version stays 1, and exactly one tier list exists in src/ (schema.ts), imported by config.ts: Diff adds TIERS const to schema.ts and floor: z.enum(TIERS).optional() + source: z.string().optional() to routingHints; config.ts imports TIERS and derives TierEnum from it. Verified: grep for the array literal ["cheap", "mid", "frontier"] in src/ hits only src/graph/schema.ts:6; individual tier strings elsewhere are config values/error text, not a second tier list. Tests for accept-floor/source, reject-ultra, and backward-compat (absent routingHints, existing pin/escalate shapes) all pass.
✓ validateGraph accepts routingHints { floor: 'mid', source: '02-01-PLAN.md' } and rejects { floor: 'ultra' } with GraphValidationError — npx vitest run tests/graph exits 0: Ran npx vitest run tests/graph: 15/15 tests pass, including the new accepts-floor-mid, accepts-floor+source, and rejects-floor-ultra (GraphValidationError) cases.
✓ RunGraphSchema version field is still z.literal(1); no version-2 literal: grep -c 'z.literal(1)' src/graph/schema.ts returns 1; no z.literal(2) exists; new test asserts version:2 throws GraphValidationError and passes.
✓ tests/fixtures/graph-v1.3.json contains no routingHints, passes validateGraph, and a fake-adapter resume-replay run completes without re-dispatching the done task — npx vitest run tests/run/fixture-resume.test.ts exits 0: grep routingHints on the fixture finds nothing; ran the test file: both tests pass — fixture validates unchanged, and after journal replay marks P01-01 done, the daemon completes with only P01-02 dispatched (P01-01 task-dispatch count stays 1, its FakeAdapter script would exit 1 if run). Fake adapter only, zero tokens.
✓ schema/rungraph.schema.json regenerated with floor enum cheap/mid/frontier; second npm run schema produces zero git diff: Diff shows the committed schema.json gains the floor enum [cheap, mid, frontier] and source string under routingHints. Ran npm run schema in the worktree: git diff/status on schema/ is empty afterward — the artifact is deterministic and matches the extended TaskSchema (the id-pattern change also reflects the current TaskSchema, i.e. the artifact was stale before regeneration).
✓ config.ts derives TierEnum from TIERS imported from ../graph/schema.js — exactly one tier literal list in src/: config.ts:6 imports { TIERS } from "../graph/schema.js" and config.ts:8 defines TierEnum = z.enum(TIERS, ...); the tier array literal exists only in schema.ts.

data.notes: Not a task problem — cursor-agent never started working. The transcript shows it blocked on an interactive 'Workspace Trust Required' dialog for the fresh worktree; despite --force it sat waiting for a keypress until the 15m stall fired. P02-02 failed the identical way at the same moment, so this is systemic to the cursor-agent adapter on new worktrees, not P02-03's content. Reroute to a different channel (claude-code or opencode) to unblock now, and file a harness fix: the cursor adapter should pre-trust the worktree (send the 'a' keystroke, or set cursor's trusted-directories config for .tickmarkr/worktrees) before dispatching any more cursor tasks.

Not a task failure — the worker never started. cursor-agent is blocked on its interactive 'Workspace Trust Required' prompt for the fresh worktree and sat there the full 15m; no tokens were spent on the task itself. Retrying on the same CLI will hit the identical prompt on every new worktree. Reroute P02-02 to a different adapter (claude-code:sonnet or opencode) which has no trust gate. Note for the harness: P02-03 is also dispatched on cursor-agent and will stall the same way; the cursor-agent adapter needs to pre-trust worktrees (e.g. auto-answer the trust prompt or a --force/trust flag covering new directories) before it's usable for dispatch.

data.summary: compileOne now parses routing.floor/routing.pin frontmatter intoschema-valid, fail-closed Task.routingHints with a plan-basename source stamp, verified by 7 new testsplus full green suite and build.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/compile/gsd.ts        | 31 +++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 tests/compile/gsd.test.ts | 57 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 87 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

✓ GSD frontmatter routing hints compile into schema-valid Task.routingHints with fail-closed validation and a plan-basename source stamp; hint-less plans are byte-identical to v1.3 output.: compileOne validates routing frontmatter fail-closed (non-object routing, bad tier, partial pin all throw CompileError), stamps source with basename(file), and emits routingHints via conditional spread so hint-less tasks are unchanged; compileGsd output passes validateGraph (all 23 tests green), proving schema validity.
✓ Compiling a GSD plan whose frontmatter carries routing.floor: mid yields a task with routingHints.floor === 'mid' and routingHints.source equal to the plan file's basename — npx vitest run tests/compile/gsd.test.ts exits 0: Test '17-floor' asserts routingHints toEqual { floor: 'mid', source: '17-01-PLAN.md' }; verified: npx vitest run tests/compile/gsd.test.ts exits 0 with 23/23 passing.
✓ Compiling a plan whose frontmatter carries routing.pin with via and model yields a task with routingHints.pin { via, model } and a stamped source: Test '18-pin' asserts routingHints toEqual { pin: { via: 'claude-code', model: 'fable' }, source: '18-01-PLAN.md' }; implementation requires both via and model to be strings before emitting pin.
✓ A plan with routing.floor set to a non-tier value fails compile with CompileError whose message names the plan file AND lists the valid tiers cheap, mid, frontier: Implementation throws CompileError with `${file} has routing.floor "..." — valid tiers are ${TIERS.join(", ")}`; test '20-badtier' asserts CompileError, /20-01-PLAN\.md/, and /cheap, mid, frontier/ — all pass.
✓ A plan whose routing.pin is missing via or model fails compile with CompileError naming the plan file: Pin with non-string via or model throws CompileError including the file path (also covers pin: null / non-object pin via isPlainObject guard); test '21-partialpin' asserts CompileError and the filename — passes.
✓ A plan with no routing frontmatter compiles to a task object that has NO routingHints key at all (not an empty object): routingHints stays undefined when fm.routing is undefined (and even when routing has neither floor nor pin), and the conditional spread `...(routingHints ? { routingHints } : {})` omits the key entirely; test '23-none' asserts `'routingHints' in g.tasks[0]` is false — passes.

data.summary: Threaded routingHints.floor into route()'s minTier as a hard >=constraint, split the pin path so task-hint pins degrade-with-lint (miss or below-floor) while config mappins still throw, added Route.provenance and wired it into tickmarkr plan, with full test coverage acrossrouter/CLI/daemon layers.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/plan.ts   |  4 +--
 src/cli/commands/run.ts    |  2 +-
 src/route/router.ts        | 38 +++++++++++++++++++++------
 tests/cli/cli.test.ts      | 44 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/route/router.test.ts | 65 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts   | 30 +++++++++++++++++++++
 6 files changed, 168 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)

✓ Task floor is >= narrowing in all paths, planner pins try-first with lint-and-degrade, operator map pins throw, every Route carries greppable provenance: route() computes minTier = maxTier(base, taskFloor) as a hard >= filter on the eligible set; the task-hint pin path is split out (find + degrade with lint on miss or below-floor, no throw) while the config map pin path keeps resolvePin's RoutingError; all three return paths construct a non-empty provenance string. Failover stays inside the floor by construction since nextChannel is untouched and the initial pick is already >= floor.
✓ Provenance visible in tickmarkr plan, pin-miss lints reach the journal through the untouched daemon seam, strict refusal tested, hinted task still fails over: plan.ts appends '— ${r.provenance}' per task line; src/run/daemon.ts has no hunks in the diff yet the new daemon test asserts a routing-lint journal event for the pin-miss task (existing lint seam); --route-strict refusal is locked by a new cli test; the floor:'mid' quota-exhaustion daemon test proves failover (quota-failover fake-1→fake-2) still fires.
✓ routingHints.floor 'mid' on a cheap-floored shape routes to TIER_RANK >= mid (router tests): New test 'task floor narrows eligible set as >=' uses shape chore (cheap floor) with floor:'mid' and asserts TIER_RANK[r.assignment.tier] >= TIER_RANK.mid; implementation's maxTier(base, taskFloor) + eligible filter delivers it. A second test locks max-not-??-fallback (frontier floor beats implement's mid map tier).
✓ Task-hint pin to an absent channel does not throw; lint matches /unavailable/ with source; provenance matches /unavailable/: Task pin path uses channels.find (no resolvePin), on miss pushes lint 'pinned gemini:flash unavailable — degrading to floor/auto (source)' and sets degradeNote 'pin gemini:flash unavailable → ' prefixed onto the applied provenance; test asserts the lint regex, the '02-03-PLAN.md' source, and /unavailable/ in provenance.
✓ Config routing.map pin to an absent channel still throws RoutingError (locking test): entry?.pin still goes through resolvePin (fail-loud); new test 'map pin to an absent channel still throws (operator-authored)' locks it with a cloned config pinning gemini:flash on shape plan.
✓ Task pin below the task's own floor degrades like a pin miss; initial pick and failover stay inside the floor: belowFloor = c && taskFloor && TIER_RANK[c.tier] < TIER_RANK[taskFloor] routes the pin into the same lint-and-degrade branch with reason 'below floor <tier>'; the degraded path then applies minTier = max(base, taskFloor) so the initial pick is >= floor, and failover inherits that by construction (hint-blind nextChannel over an already-floored pick). Test A3 asserts tier >= frontier, the pin lint, and both provenance segments.
✓ nextChannel is hint-blind (0 routingHints matches in its body) and a floored task with quota exhaustion still fails over at/above the floor in a fake-adapter daemon run: The diff touches nothing in nextChannel — every routingHints reference added lives inside route(); the new daemon test dispatches a floor:'mid' task whose first channel quota-fails and asserts done=['T1'] plus a quota-failover event fake:fake-1 → fake:fake-2, so hints did not freeze the ladder.
✓ Pin-miss during a fake-adapter daemon run produces a routing-lint journal event with zero changes to src/run/daemon.ts: git diff contains no src/run/daemon.ts hunks; the new daemon test pins T1 to absent gemini:flash, asserts the task still completes, finds a routing-lint event for T1 in the journal, and matches /unavailable/ in its data — the lint flows through the pre-existing daemon journaling seam.
✓ tickmarkr run --route-strict refuses to dispatch on a pin-miss lint before any task runs; non-strict runs journal the lint and execute: run.ts's strict pre-check (route all tasks, throw on any lints) now fires for pin-miss lints since they land in Route.lints; the new cli test asserts rejects.toThrow(/refusing to dispatch/) and that .tickmarkr/runs was never created; the non-strict path is covered by the daemon pin-miss test where T1 executes and the lint is journaled.
✓ Route carries provenance: string; tickmarkr plan prints a provenance segment per task and output never contains 'undefined': Route interface gains provenance: string and all three return paths populate it from guarded string pieces (src ternary, reason only when defined, degradeNote defaults to ''); plan.ts prints it per line; tests assert a /—\s+(pin|floor|map tier|auto)/ segment and not.toContain('undefined') across auto, map-tier, pin, and pin-miss paths.
review output unparseable (reviewer cursor-agent:composer-2.5) — failing closed

data.summary: Threaded routingHints.floor into route() as a hard >= constraint (allpaths), split pin resolution so task-hint pins degrade-with-lint on miss/below-floor while config map pinsstill throw, added Route.provenance surfaced in tickmarkr plan, and added the full router/CLI/daemon testmatrix — all green with daemon.ts untouched.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
src/cli/commands/plan.ts   |  4 +--
 src/cli/commands/run.ts    |  2 +-
 src/route/router.ts        | 61 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---------
 tests/cli/cli.test.ts      | 43 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/route/router.test.ts | 46 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts   | 33 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 6 files changed, 172 insertions(+), 17 deletions(-)

✓ Task floor is >= narrowing in all paths, planner pins try-first with lint-and-degrade, operator map pins throw, every Route carries greppable provenance: route() computes minTier = maxTier(entry.tier ?? cfgFloor ?? cheap, taskFloor); task pin branch degrades with lint on miss/below-floor and re-enters the floored auto path; map pin branch still calls resolvePin (throws); Route interface gains provenance: string populated on every return path.
✓ Provenance visible in tickmarkr plan, pin-miss lints reach journal via untouched daemon seam, strict-mode refusal tested, hinted task demonstrably fails over (HINT-04/05, SC-2/3/4/5 executable): plan.ts appends `— ${r.provenance}` per task line; daemon.ts:104 pre-existing routing-lint journal seam is unchanged in the diff; --route-strict refusal and quota-failover-above-floor are both covered by new tests, all 38 tests in the three touched suites pass.
✓ Task with routingHints.floor 'mid' on a cheap-floored shape routes to tier >= mid; router tests exit 0: Test 'routingHints.floor narrows minTier as a hard constraint (>=)' uses shape chore (cheap floor) with floor mid and asserts TIER_RANK >= TIER_RANK.mid; npx vitest run tests/route/router.test.ts passes (verified, exit 0).
✓ Task-hint pin to absent channel does not throw: assignment returned, lint matches /unavailable/ carrying hint source, provenance matches /unavailable/: Task pin branch uses channels.find (no throw); lint line includes `${reason}${src}` where reason='unavailable' and src carries routingHints.source; provenance is prefixed with the degraded string containing 'unavailable'; locking test asserts all three.
✓ Config routing.map pin to absent channel still throws RoutingError (locking test): Map pin path still goes through resolvePin which throws RoutingError; new test 'config routing.map pin to an absent channel still throws (operator fail-loud, D-05)' locks it.
✓ Task pin below the task's own floor degrades like a pin miss — initial pick and failover stay inside the floor: belowTaskFloor check (TIER_RANK[c.tier] < TIER_RANK[taskFloor]) routes to the same lint+degrade path; test A3 asserts pin unused, assignment tier >= frontier, and lint matches /below task floor/; floor is folded into minTier so the auto path stays inside it.
✓ nextChannel is hint-blind (0 routingHints matches in its body) and floored task with quota exhaustion fails over to a channel at/above the floor in a fake-adapter daemon run: Extracting the nextChannel function body and grepping for routingHints returns 0 matches (verified); daemon test 'floored task fails over on quota exhaustion' passes with both task-dispatch events at mid/frontier.
✓ Pin-miss during a fake-adapter daemon run produces a routing-lint journal event with zero changes to src/run/daemon.ts: The diff contains no hunk for src/run/daemon.ts; the pre-existing seam at daemon.ts:104 journals route lints; new daemon test finds a routing-lint event for T1 matching /unavailable/ and the task still completes.
✓ tickmarkr run --route-strict refuses to dispatch on a pin-miss lint before any task runs; non-strict runs journal and execute: run.ts strict check routes all tasks and throws 'refusing to dispatch' on any lint before runDaemon; CLI test asserts rejection, and the daemon pin-miss test proves non-strict runs journal the lint and complete normally.
✓ Route carries provenance: string; tickmarkr plan prints a provenance segment per task and its output never contains 'undefined': Route interface includes provenance: string; plan output line appends it; CLI test asserts a provenance segment regex and not.toContain('undefined'); router test sweeps four route shapes asserting non-empty provenance without 'undefined'.
review output unparseable (reviewer opencode:opencode/kimi-k2) — failing closed

data.summary: Task floor now hard-narrows route() in all paths, task pins degradewith journaled lints while map pins stay fail-loud, every Route carries provenance printed by tickmarkr plan,and strict/failover/daemon-seam behavior is locked by 197 green zero-tokentests.

data.note: operator-cleared: manual merge into milestone after diagnosis — all objective gates green (build/test/197, evidence, scope) + acceptance passed twice; review gate unparseable across 3 vendors due to self-reference trap (diff contains TICKMARKR_EXIT:/lint strings). Merge commit e8aca43.
